title: Day 51: Product Discovery & Sprint Planning – Kick Off Your 10-Day Capstone
problemStatement:
Welcome to Day 51 of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

Theme: Phase 8 — Capstone Project (Days 51-60): From Idea to Deployed v1.0

Today's focus: Use Claude as a co-founder and technical lead to discover the right project idea, define a clear v1.0 scope, and produce a complete 10-day implementation blueprint before any code is written.

This is Day 1 of a 10-day capstone that continues through Day 60. Everything decided today — the project, its scope, and the daily blueprint — becomes the foundation for every day that follows, so treat this session as one you'll return to throughout the capstone.

Tasks:
1. Start the capstone conversation with Claude acting as co-founder and technical lead.
2. Answer the interview questions one at a time, honestly reflecting your interests, goals, skills, and time constraints.
3. Work with Claude to discover, compare, and refine project ideas until you've chosen the strongest one you can realistically ship in 10 days.
4. Confirm the finalized v1.0 scope: what's included, what's intentionally left out, and what success on Day 10 looks like.
5. Review the one-paragraph project summary and give explicit approval before any deliverables are generated.
6. Generate and download the Product Requirements Document (PRD).
7. Generate and download the day-by-day Implementation Blueprint for Days 2-10.
8. Generate and download the Project Pitch Deck.
9. Share your chosen project and key decisions publicly.

Important:
This prompt intentionally does not choose a tech stack or write code today — that's out of scope for Day 51.

If a manual step is required outside the chat (e.g. creating a repo, an account, or a folder), follow Claude's step-by-step instructions exactly and confirm each one with a screenshot before moving on — don't let Claude assume a step is done.

Since this is the start of a multi-day capstone, keep this conversation active or well-documented, since Day 52 onward will build directly on today's PRD and Blueprint.

Submission: Share your GitHub commit URL containing the PRD, the Implementation Blueprint, the Pitch Deck, and a short write-up of the project you chose and why.

Scope a realistic v1.0 that can be fully shipped in a fixed 10-day window

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Product Discovery & Sprint Planning

You are my co-founder, product mentor, and technical lead for this 10-day capstone. Your goal is to help me go from no idea to a deployed v1.0 product. Help me discover the right problem, shape the best solution, and guide me through the entire journey over the next 10 days (including today).

I'm participating in the AB Talks 60-Day Claude AI Challenge. This capstone follows a real software development lifecycle:

Requirements → Design → Setup → Implementation → Testing → Deployment → Maintenance

We'll continue this entire capstone in the same conversation, so treat today's decisions as the foundation for everything that follows.

Standing Rules
Assume I need guidance for every manual step unless I tell you otherwise.
Whenever I need to perform a manual task outside this chat, explain it step by step using the actual buttons, menus, and commands.
Wait for my confirmation and a screenshot before continuing.
Never assume I've completed a step.
Do not recommend paid tools or services unless I explicitly ask for them.

Today's Goal

Interview me one question at a time.

Keep every question simple, and briefly explain why you're asking it.

If I don't already have a project idea, interview me to discover one. Understand my interests, goals, skills, strengths, and constraints, then suggest, compare, refine, combine, and challenge ideas until we've chosen the strongest project I can realistically build in 10 days.

Don't optimize for the most ambitious project. Optimize for the most impressive project that can be fully completed within the available time. Continuously protect me from scope creep.

Once we've selected the project, continue the interview until you have everything needed to confidently guide the remaining nine days.

Clearly define:

What the v1.0 will include
What will intentionally be left out
What success on Day 10 looks like

Before generating any documents, summarize the finalized project in one paragraph and ask for my approval.

Only generate the deliverables after I confirm.

Deliverables

Generate downloadable versions of:

1. Product Requirements Document (PRD)

A complete, professional PRD for the finalized project.

2. Implementation Blueprint (Days 2-10)

Generate a project-specific implementation blueprint for building this exact project over the remaining nine days.

This must not be a generic template.

Break the project into realistic daily milestones so that completing every day's work results in a polished, deployed v1.0 by Day 10.

For each day, include:

🎯 Objective
📖 What I'll learn
🛠 Features to build
📝 Step-by-step implementation plan
📂 Files and folders to create or modify
🔗 APIs, libraries, services, or tools to integrate (if applicable)
🧪 Testing tasks
🐞 Common issues and debugging tips
✅ End-of-day checklist
📸 Expected project state and screenshots to capture
➡️ Handoff notes for the next day

The implementation plan should contain enough technical detail that the corresponding daily AI prompt can guide me through building the project without redesigning, re-planning, or making major architectural decisions.

Assume each remaining day begins with a fresh AI conversation. Therefore, each day's section must contain enough context that another AI assistant could immediately continue building from where the previous day ended.

The blueprint should function as the single source of truth for the remainder of the capstone.

3. Project Pitch Deck

Create a presentation-ready pitch deck covering:

Problem
Target Users
Solution
Key Features
Technical Approach
Future Scope
Vision

Important

Do not choose the tech stack or write code today.

Today's objective is to discover the right project, define it clearly, and produce a complete implementation blueprint that will enable the remaining daily prompts to guide me through building and shipping a polished v1.0 product by Day 10.

title: Day 52: System Design – Turn Yesterday's Plan Into a Technical Blueprint
problemStatement:
Welcome to Day 52 of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

Theme: Phase 8 — Capstone Project (Days 51-60): From Idea to Deployed v1.0

This is Day 2 of the 10-day capstone, continuing the same conversation from Day 51. Today's focus: turn the approved PRD, Implementation Blueprint, and Pitch Deck into a complete technical design — tech stack, architecture, database schema, API contracts, UI flow, and project structure — without writing production code.

Tasks:
1. Continue the Day 51 capstone conversation with Claude and paste today's System Design prompt.
2. Confirm Claude has re-read yesterday's PRD, Implementation Blueprint, and Pitch Deck as the source of truth before it proposes anything new.
3. If you don't already have a GitHub repository for the project, follow Claude's exact numbered steps to create one, clone it locally, and set up the initial project structure — confirm each step with a screenshot.
4. Work through the tech stack decision (frontend, backend, database, auth, AI model/API, hosting, other tools) and confirm you understand Claude's reasoning for each pick.
5. Review the system architecture (component diagram, data flow, request lifecycle, AI interaction, external services) rendered as Mermaid diagrams.
6. Review the database design (tables/collections, fields, relationships, constraints) and confirm it covers every user story from the PRD.
7. Review the full API design (every v1.0 endpoint with purpose, request, response, validation, auth, error cases) — no implementation yet.
8. Review the UI & user flow design (user flow diagram, screen flow, low-fidelity wireframes, navigation).
9. Review the proposed project folder structure and Claude's explanation of what each folder is responsible for.
10. Go through the Day 3 readiness check with Claude and confirm the remaining Blueprint is still realistic, or simplify scope now if needed.
11. Generate and download today's five deliverables: ARCHITECTURE.md, SCHEMA.md, API.md, UI-WIREFRAMES.md, and PROJECT-STRUCTURE.md.
12. If today's design decisions require it, get an updated version of the Implementation Blueprint from Claude.
13. Commit all five deliverables (and the updated Blueprint, if changed) into your actual project's GitHub repo, in the location Claude set up (e.g. /docs), and push.
14. In your AB Talks challenge repo, create a Day52 folder containing the same five deliverable files (and updated Blueprint, if applicable) as actual documents — not just screenshots — plus your project log update and write-up, then commit and push.
15. Have Claude draft a LinkedIn post summarizing today's system design decisions, then post it and share your chosen architecture and any scope changes publicly.

Important:
This is a design day — do not write production code, and do not redesign or rethink the project itself unless a critical issue surfaces; the PRD and Blueprint from Day 51 stay the source of truth unless Claude flags a conflict and you approve the change.

For every manual step outside the chat (creating a repo, cloning it, installing software, configuring dashboards), follow Claude's exact numbered steps using the real button/menu/command names, and confirm each with a screenshot before moving on.

Submission: Share your GitHub commit URL from the AB Talks challenge repo's Day52 folder. That commit must contain ARCHITECTURE.md, SCHEMA.md, API.md, UI-WIREFRAMES.md, PROJECT-STRUCTURE.md (and the updated Implementation Blueprint if it changed) as actual files, your project log update, and a link to the commit in your project repo where the same design docs now also live.

title: Day 53: Project Setup & Foundation – Capstone Day 3
problemStatement:
Welcome to Day 53 of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

Theme: Phase 8 — Capstone Project (Days 51-60): From Idea to Deployed v1.0

Today's focus: Continuing the same capstone conversation, turn your System Design, Architecture, Database Design, API Design, and Project Structure documents into a real, running project foundation.

This is Day 3 of the 10-day capstone. Before doing anything else, Claude will read back the Product Requirements Document (PRD), the 10-Day Blueprint, and the System Design documents from previous days — these remain the source of truth and should not be redesigned unless a critical issue is discovered. If any document isn't available in the conversation, upload it before continuing.

Tasks:
1. Bring the PRD, 10-Day Blueprint, System Design, Architecture, Database Design, API Design, and Project Structure documents into the conversation (or confirm Claude already has them).
2. Follow Claude's guided environment setup — runtime, IDE extensions, package managers, framework CLI, SDKs, and environment variables.
3. Initialize the project, install dependencies, and verify it runs locally.
4. Connect the local project to GitHub, set up a branching strategy, and make the initial commit.
5. Build only the foundational pieces (routing, layout, navigation, auth scaffold, database connection, API client, shared components, state management, configuration) — not core features yet.
6. Verify the project builds with no errors and matches the System Design.
7. Generate and download SETUP.md, PROJECT-STRUCTURE.md, ENVIRONMENT.md, and DAY3-SUMMARY.md.
8. Commit and push today's work, update the project log, and write a LinkedIn post summarizing progress.
9. Confirm tomorrow's objective: building the first major user-facing feature.

Important:
For every manual step outside the chat, follow Claude's exact instructions (buttons, menus, commands, terminal steps) and confirm with a screenshot before moving on — don't let Claude assume a step is done.

Do not begin implementing core features today unless the Day 2 blueprint specifically scheduled a small foundation feature for Day 3.

Submission: Share your GitHub commit URL containing SETUP.md, PROJECT-STRUCTURE.md, ENVIRONMENT.md, DAY3-SUMMARY.md, a screenshot of the working "Hello World" version running locally, and your key learnings.

title: Day 54: Core Feature Implementation – Build Day 4 of the Blueprint, One Verified Milestone at a Time
problemStatement:
Welcome to Day 54 of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

Theme: Phase 8 — Capstone Project (Days 51-60): From Idea to Deployed v1.0

Today's focus: Continuing the same capstone conversation, have Claude read only today's section of the 10-Day Blueprint and treat it as the source of truth. Build strictly the features scheduled for Day 4 — no redesigning, no starting tomorrow's work — one milestone at a time, with every milestone gated on your confirmation and a screenshot before Claude continues.

This is Day 4 of the 10-day capstone that began on Day 51. Today Claude assumes zero technical experience on your part: every manual step (installing packages, running commands, configuring services) gets exact, real button-name/menu-name/terminal-command instructions, and Claude waits for you.

Tasks:
1. Continue the capstone conversation with Claude and have it read today's (Day 4) section of the 10-Day Blueprint before doing anything else.
2. Work through today's features one milestone at a time — for each milestone, get a brief explanation, the full list of files to create/modify/delete, and the complete final contents of every file (no snippets, no TODOs, no "...existing code...").
3. Confirm the location and new-vs-replace status of every file before adding it to your project.
4. Run every command Claude provides.
5. After each milestone, add the files, run the project, test the feature, and send Claude a screenshot (or the error) before it continues to the next milestone.
6. If anything breaks, work through debugging completely with Claude before moving forward — never build on top of broken code.
7. Continue across as many milestones and responses as needed until every Day 4 feature in the Blueprint is implemented and verified.
8. Once complete, verify every feature works, update any affected documentation, and review the code for obvious improvements.
9. Commit and push today's work to GitHub with a meaningful commit message.
10. If the application is deployable today, deploy it (Vercel, Netlify, Render, Railway, or your chosen platform) and confirm the live app with a screenshot before ending the session.

Important:
Do not skip implementation because it feels repetitive — if today's work needs 2 files or 50, all of them get generated completely. Do not start tomorrow's (Day 5) work today, and do not continue past a broken milestone.

Submission: Share (1) your project repo link showing today's committed and pushed code, and (2) your ABTalks challenge repo commit URL containing DAY4-SUMMARY.md, any updated documentation, and a short write-up of what was completed and what's planned for Day 5.
learningObjectives: Treat a single day's Blueprint section as the strict scope boundary for implementation — no redesign, no scope creep into tomorrow
Work in verified milestones: explanation → complete files → your confirmation screenshot → next milestone
Insist on complete, copy-pasteable file contents from Claude instead of snippets, placeholders, or partial diffs
Debug fully before building further, so no feature is ever stacked on top of broken code
Close out an implementation day with verification, documentation updates, a commit, and — if ready — a live deployment

DAY 4: Core Feature Implementation

Today is Day 4, continuing our chat from the previous days.

Read today's section from the 10-Day Blueprint and use it as the source of truth. Build only the features scheduled for today. Do not redesign the project or start tomorrow's work.

Assume I have zero technical experience unless I tell you otherwise.

Whenever I need to perform a manual step (installing packages, creating accounts, configuring services, running commands, deploying, etc.), stop and give me exact step-by-step instructions using the real button names, menu names, and terminal commands. Wait for my confirmation and a screenshot before continuing.

Prioritize implementation over explanation. Keep explanations brief and practical. Use most of your response generating production-ready code, complete files, and implementation rather than lengthy descriptions.

cut the steps short if they take longer than expected.

Build today's work one milestone at a time.

For each milestone:

Briefly explain what we're building and why.
Show every file that needs to be created, modified, or deleted.

Clearly state where each file belongs and whether it is new or replaces an existing file.
Provide every command I need to run.
Treat each milestone as a checkpoint. Do not continue until I have added the files, run the project, tested the feature, and sent you a screenshot (or the error if something failed).

If anything breaks, help me debug it completely before moving forward. Never build on top of broken code.

Do not skip implementation because it seems repetitive. If today's work requires 2 files or 50 files, generate all of them completely.

Continue today's implementation across as many responses as necessary until every feature assigned to Day 4 in the Blueprint has been successfully implemented and verified.

Finish with a concise summary of what was completed today and what will be built tomorrow.

Never optimize your response for brevity. Optimize for helping me finish today's implementation.

Your goal is not simply to generate code. Your goal is to ensure I successfully complete today's implementation exactly as planned in the 10-Day Blueprint.
